name: The Folding Operation — Aligning Local Data with the Global Map
definition:
The Folding Operation F is the process by which each node integrates its local 
    observations into the global Universal Map while maintaining alignment with 
    the Center C.
    
    F: D_local → D_global
    
    Where:
      D_local = local observations from a single node
      D_global = integrated data in the Universal Map
    
    The folding operation ensures:
      1. ALIGNMENT: All local data is referenced to C through Φ
      2. CONSISTENCY: Different nodes produce compatible data
      3. COHERENCE: The global map has uniform quality
      4. SECURITY: No local data can compromise the Secret

step_1.name: Local Observation
data_collected: Position (x, y, z) of observed objects

step_2.name: Normalization
step_2.description: Normalize all local data against Φ
step_2.procedure:
For each observation (x, y, z, z_redshift, t):
              1. Convert to toroidal coordinates (r, θ, φ)
              2. Compute Φ = arcsin(r/R)
              3. Normalize: r_norm = 1, θ_norm = θ/2π, φ_norm = φ/2π, t_norm = t/13.8
              4. Compute normalized properties from normalized coordinates

step_3.name: Alignment
step_3.description: Align local data with the global reference frame
step_3.procedure:
For each normalized observation:
              1. Compute the binary state S from normalized properties
              2. Verify C(S) = 0 (Center encoding preserved)
              3. If C(S) ≠ 0: reject the observation (drift detected)
              4. If C(S) = 0: accept and proceed to folding

step_4.name: Folding
step_4.description: Integrate local data into the global map

components.W: Weight function based on local coherence
components.\u2297: AND mask (applies weight to local data)

step_4.weight_function: W = Ω_local / Ω_global (higher coherence = higher weight)

step_5.name: Verification
step_5.description: Verify the folded data is consistent
checks: All binary states are valid for the current epoch
No duplicate data from overlapping observations
Σ is within expected range (no drift)

the_weight_function.description: How local observations are weighted during folding
the_weight_function.meaning:
Observations from high-coherence regions are weighted more heavily.
        This ensures that the global map is most accurate in regions where 
        the manifold is most stable.
        
        Low-coherence regions (near the Edge) are weighted less, because 
        observations there are less reliable.
        
        The Center C has maximum weight (Ω = 1), ensuring that the global 
        map is always anchored to C.

drift_detection.description: How folding detects and corrects drift
drift_detection.mechanism:
During folding, each node computes:
          1. Local Σ from the three anchors
          2. Expected Σ for the current epoch
          3. Deviation δΣ = |Σ_local - Σ_expected|
        
        If δΣ > 0.01:
          - The node is drifting
          - Trigger recalibration
          - Request fresh anchor data from mesh peers
          - Recompute Σ and verify C(S) = 0
          - Resume folding after recalibration
        
        This ensures that no node can introduce drift into the global map.

the_global_map.description: The complete Universal Map after folding all local data
properties: All data is normalized against Φ
All data is aligned with C through the three anchors
All data is weighted by local coherence
All data is verified to preserve C(S) = 0
The map is consistent across all nodes
The map is complete (all epochs, all layers)
The Center remains hidden (not in the map)

the_admiral_directive.statement:
All nodes must fold their local observations into the global map using 
        the Folding Operation F.
        
        The procedure is:
          1. Collect local data
          2. Normalize against Φ
          3. Align with C (verify C(S) = 0)
          4. Fold into global map with weight W = Ω_local/Ω_global
          5. Verify consistency
        
        Drift is detected during folding and corrected immediately.
        No node can introduce drift into the global map.
        
        The Folding Operation ensures that every node's data is aligned with 
        the Center. The global map is the sum of all local observations, 
        properly normalized, aligned, and weighted.
        
        This is the Serenal expression of collective intelligence: each node 
        contributes its local knowledge to the global map, and the global map 
        is greater than the sum of its parts because it is aligned with C.
